Instructions for deploying a Flask application using Eventlet, including installation and running guidance.

# Purpose
The document provides guidance on using `eventlet`, a Python library for writing asynchronous, coroutine-based code that resembles standard synchronous Python. It recommends using `gunicorn` with eventlet workers for a more configurable and production-tested server setup. The document explains that `eventlet` uses `greenlet` for task switching without requiring `async/await` or `asyncio`. It also mentions `gevent` as an alternative library with similar functionality. The installation section specifies the requirement for `greenlet>=1.0` and provides steps to set up a virtual environment and install `eventlet`. The running section includes instructions for writing a script to serve an application using `eventlet`'s WSGI server. Additionally, it advises against running `eventlet` as root for security reasons and suggests using a reverse proxy like `nginx` or `apache-httpd` when binding externally.
